steps.title: Identify the solution as the intersection of the two lines
steps.content: The solution to a system of linear equations is the point where both lines meet on the graph. This point satisfies both equations simultaneously.

steps.title: Read the coordinates of the intersection point
steps.content: On the graph, the two lines intersect at the point where $x = 2$ (two units to the right of the origin) and $y = 3$ (three units above the origin). Therefore, the solution is $(2, 3)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
